NEXT MAJOR SECTION

SPRING FRAMEWORK + SPRING BOOT DEEP DIVE

This will be significantly larger than Java. Expected Coverage: * IOC * Dependency Injection * Bean Lifecycle * Spring Boot Internals * Auto Configuration * Spring MVC * REST APIs * Validation * Exception Handling * Spring Data JPA * Hibernate * Transactions * Caching * Profiles * Configuration * Actuator * Testing Expected Questions: Q159 - Q350+

SECTION 28 - SPRING FRAMEWORK FUNDAMENTALS

This is arguably the highest ROI section for a Java Backend Engineer. For Spring Boot interviews, most interviewers eventually trace everything back to: * IOC * Dependency Injection * Bean Lifecycle * Spring Container If you understand these deeply, many advanced Spring questions become easier.

Q159. What Is Spring Framework?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Spring Framework is a comprehensive Java framework that simplifies enterprise application development. It provides:

  • Dependency Injection
  • Inversion Of Control
  • Transaction Management
  • MVC Framework
  • Security
  • Data Access
  • AOP

Why Spring Became Popular?

Before Spring, enterprise Java development often relied heavily on EJBs which were complex and difficult to maintain. Spring simplified development through lightweight POJOs and dependency injection.

Q160. What Is IOC (Inversion Of Control)?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

IOC means the responsibility of creating and managing objects is transferred from application code to the Spring container.

Without Spring


UserService service =
new UserService();

Application creates dependency.

With Spring


@Autowired

UserService service;

Spring creates and injects dependency.

Interview Gold Line

"Inversion of Control means objects do not manage their own dependencies; the container manages them."

Q161. What Is Dependency Injection?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Dependency Injection is a design pattern where dependencies are provided from outside rather than being created inside the class.

Bad Example


class UserService {

    private UserRepository repo =
        new UserRepository();

}

Good Example


class UserService {

    private UserRepository repo;

}

Spring injects the dependency.

Benefits

  • Loose Coupling
  • Testability
  • Maintainability
Q162. Types Of Dependency Injection

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Type Status
Constructor Injection Recommended
Setter Injection Optional Dependencies
Field Injection Avoid
Q163. Why Constructor Injection Is Preferred?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer

  • Immutable Dependencies
  • Easier Unit Testing
  • No Null Dependencies
  • Explicit Design

Senior Insight

Most modern Spring Boot projects use constructor injection exclusively.

Q164. What Is A Spring Bean?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

A Spring Bean is an object managed by the Spring IOC container.

Examples


@Service

@Repository

@Component

@Controller

Spring creates, configures and manages these objects.

Q165. What Is ApplicationContext?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer

ApplicationContext is the central Spring container responsible for managing beans and application configuration.

Responsibilities

  • Create Beans
  • Inject Dependencies
  • Manage Lifecycle
  • Handle Events
Q166. BeanFactory vs ApplicationContext

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

BeanFactory ApplicationContext
Basic Container Advanced Container
Lazy Loading Additional Features
Minimal Enterprise Usage

Interview Gold Answer

ApplicationContext is built on top of BeanFactory and is used in almost all modern Spring applications.

SECTION 29 - SPRING BEAN LIFECYCLE

Q167. Explain Spring Bean Lifecycle

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer


Bean Instantiation

↓

Dependency Injection

↓

@PostConstruct

↓

Bean Ready

↓

Application Running

↓

@PreDestroy

↓

Bean Destroyed
Q168. What Is @PostConstruct?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Answer

Method executed after dependency injection is completed.

Use Cases

  • Cache Initialization
  • Configuration Loading
  • Startup Validation
Q169. What Is @PreDestroy?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Executed before bean destruction.

Use Cases

  • Close Connections
  • Cleanup Resources
  • Flush Buffers
Q170. What Is Bean Scope?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Answer

Bean scope determines bean lifecycle and visibility.

Q171. Common Bean Scopes

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Scope Description
Singleton One Bean Per Container
Prototype New Bean Every Request
Request Per HTTP Request
Session Per User Session
Q172. Default Bean Scope?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Answer


Singleton

Important

Most Spring beans are singletons.

Q173. Are Singleton Beans Thread Safe?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Interview Ready Answer

No.

Singleton scope only means one instance exists. It does NOT guarantee thread safety.

Example


@Service

public class CounterService {

    int count = 0;

}

Multiple requests may modify count simultaneously.

Q174. How To Make Singleton Beans Safe?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Strong Answer

  • Keep Beans Stateless
  • Avoid Shared Mutable State
  • Use Thread-Safe Structures
  • Use Synchronization Carefully

Production Advice

Most service beans should be stateless.

SECTION 30 - SPRING STEREOTYPE ANNOTATIONS

Q175. Difference Between @Component, @Service, @Repository And @Controller

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Annotation Purpose
@Component Generic Bean
@Service Business Logic
@Repository Data Access Layer
@Controller MVC Controller
Q176. Why Use @Service Instead Of @Component?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Interview Ready Answer

Functionally both register beans. @Service provides semantic meaning. It indicates business logic.

Benefit

Improves readability and maintainability.

Q177. Why Use @Repository?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Answer

@Repository provides exception translation. Database exceptions are converted into Spring DataAccessExceptions.

Q178. Component Scanning

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Interview Ready Answer

Spring automatically discovers beans by scanning packages.

Example


@SpringBootApplication

implicitly enables component scanning.

Q179. What Happens When Spring Starts?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Senior Answer

  1. ApplicationContext Created
  2. Configuration Loaded
  3. Component Scan Begins
  4. Beans Discovered
  5. Dependencies Injected
  6. Bean Lifecycle Executed
  7. Embedded Server Started
  8. Application Ready
Q180. Senior Follow-Up: What Happens If Spring Finds Circular Dependencies?

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Example: 


ServiceA -> ServiceB

ServiceB -> ServiceA

Spring may fail bean creation. Constructor injection exposes circular dependencies immediately.

Solution

  • Refactor Design
  • Break Dependency Chain
  • Introduce Third Service
  • Use Lazy Injection Carefully

PART 8 COMPLETE

Next Part: Q181 - Q220 Topics: * Spring Boot Internals * Auto Configuration * @SpringBootApplication * Configuration Properties * Profiles * Environment Variables * Spring Boot Startup Flow * Embedded Tomcat * Spring Boot Actuator These are among the most commonly asked Spring Boot questions in modern interviews.

SECTION 31 - SPRING BOOT FUNDAMENTALS

Many developers use Spring Boot daily but cannot explain what actually happens behind the scenes. Senior interviewers frequently ask these questions.

Q181. What Is Spring Boot?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Spring Boot is an opinionated framework built on top of Spring Framework that simplifies application development through:

  • Auto Configuration
  • Starter Dependencies
  • Embedded Servers
  • Production Ready Features

Goal

Reduce boilerplate configuration and accelerate development.

Q182. Difference Between Spring And Spring Boot

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Spring Spring Boot
Framework Built On Spring
More Configuration Auto Configuration
External Server Embedded Server
Manual Setup Convention Based
Q183. Why Is Spring Boot Popular?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Answer

  • Faster Development
  • Less Configuration
  • Embedded Tomcat
  • Auto Configuration
  • Easy Deployment
  • Production Features
Q184. What Is @SpringBootApplication?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

@SpringBootApplication is a convenience annotation combining three annotations. 


@Configuration

@ComponentScan

@EnableAutoConfiguration
Q185. Explain @Configuration

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Marks a class as a source of bean definitions.

Example


@Bean

public UserService userService()
Q186. Explain @ComponentScan

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Automatically scans packages for Spring-managed beans.

Q187. Explain @EnableAutoConfiguration

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Automatically configures Spring components based on dependencies present in the classpath.

Example


spring-boot-starter-data-jpa

Spring automatically configures:

  • EntityManager
  • DataSource
  • Transaction Manager

SECTION 32 - AUTO CONFIGURATION

Q188. What Is Auto Configuration?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Auto Configuration automatically configures beans based on:

  • Classpath Dependencies
  • Configuration Properties
  • Existing Beans
Q189. How Does Auto Configuration Work Internally?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Interview Ready Answer

@EnableAutoConfiguration triggers Spring Boot to load configuration classes from: 


META-INF/spring.factories

(Java 2.x)

META-INF/spring/org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguration.imports

(Spring Boot 3.x)

These classes contain conditional bean definitions.

Q190. What Is @ConditionalOnClass?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Loads configuration only if a specific class exists.

Example


Hibernate Present

↓

Configure JPA
Q191. What Is @ConditionalOnMissingBean?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Creates a bean only if one does not already exist.

Purpose

Allows developers to override default configurations.

Q192. How To Disable Auto Configuration?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Example


@SpringBootApplication(
exclude = {
DataSourceAutoConfiguration.class
})

SECTION 33 - SPRING BOOT STARTUP FLOW

Q193. What Happens When SpringApplication.run() Executes?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

  1. Create ApplicationContext
  2. Load Environment
  3. Load Configurations
  4. Perform Component Scan
  5. Create Beans
  6. Inject Dependencies
  7. Apply Auto Configuration
  8. Start Embedded Server
  9. Application Ready
Q194. What Is Embedded Tomcat?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Answer

Spring Boot packages Tomcat within the application.

Benefit

No separate application server installation required.

Q195. Embedded Tomcat vs External Tomcat

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Embedded External
Inside JAR Separate Server
Easy Deployment More Operational Work
Common Today Legacy Enterprise
Q196. JAR vs WAR Deployment

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

JAR WAR
Embedded Server External Server
Microservices Traditional Apps
Preferred Less Common

SECTION 34 - CONFIGURATION MANAGEMENT

Q197. application.properties vs application.yml

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Example


server.port=8080

YAML


server:
  port: 8080

Senior Insight

YAML becomes more readable for complex configurations.

Q198. What Is @Value?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Injects individual configuration values from properties files, YAML, or environment variables directly into a field or constructor parameter. It is best suited for one-off values; for grouped or structured configuration, @ConfigurationProperties is preferred.

Example


@Value("${app.name}")
Q199. What Is @ConfigurationProperties?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer

Maps configuration properties to a strongly typed Java object.

Example


app.database.url

app.database.username

mapped into a configuration class.

Why Preferred?

  • Type Safe
  • Maintainable
  • Supports Validation
Q200. @Value vs @ConfigurationProperties

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

@Value @ConfigurationProperties
Single Property Group Properties
Less Structured Type Safe
Small Configs Large Configs

Interview Gold Answer

For a few properties, @Value is acceptable. For complex configuration, prefer @ConfigurationProperties.

SECTION 35 - SPRING PROFILES

Q201. What Are Spring Profiles?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Profiles allow different configurations for different environments.

Examples


dev

qa

uat

prod
Q202. How To Activate A Profile?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High 


spring.profiles.active=prod
Q203. @Profile Annotation

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Loads beans only for specific profiles.

Example


@Profile("prod")
Q204. Why Profiles Matter In Production?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Answer

Different environments require different:

  • Database URLs
  • API Endpoints
  • Secrets
  • Logging Levels
Q205. Senior Follow-Up: How Do You Manage Secrets?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

Avoid storing secrets in source code. Use:

  • AWS Secrets Manager
  • AWS Parameter Store
  • HashiCorp Vault
  • Environment Variables
  • Kubernetes Secrets

Never commit credentials to Git repositories.

PART 9 COMPLETE

Next Part: Q206 - Q250 Topics: * Spring MVC * DispatcherServlet * REST APIs * Request Lifecycle * Validation * DTOs * Exception Handling * ResponseEntity * Content Negotiation * API Design Best Practices These questions are asked in nearly every Spring Boot interview.

SECTION 36 - SPRING MVC ARCHITECTURE

Spring MVC is one of the most frequently discussed topics because nearly every Spring Boot backend application exposes REST APIs. Senior interviewers often go beyond annotations and ask about request flow internally.

Q206. What Is Spring MVC?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Spring MVC is a web framework built on the Model-View-Controller architectural pattern. In modern backend systems, Spring MVC is commonly used to build REST APIs.

Main Components

  • DispatcherServlet
  • Controller
  • Service
  • Repository
  • View Resolver (traditional MVC)
Q207. Explain Spring MVC Request Flow

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer


Client Request

↓

DispatcherServlet

↓

Handler Mapping

↓

Controller

↓

Service Layer

↓

Repository Layer

↓

Database

↓

Response

Interview Gold Line

DispatcherServlet acts as the Front Controller and coordinates request processing.

Q208. What Is DispatcherServlet?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

DispatcherServlet is the central component of Spring MVC. It receives every incoming request and delegates processing to appropriate handlers.

Responsibilities

  • Request Routing
  • Controller Invocation
  • Exception Handling
  • Response Generation
Q209. What Is Handler Mapping?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Handler Mapping determines which controller method should handle a request.

Example


GET /users/1

↓

UserController.getUser()
Q210. What Is Handler Adapter?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Interview Ready Answer

Handler Adapter invokes the controller method selected by Handler Mapping.

It acts as a bridge between DispatcherServlet and controllers.

SECTION 37 - REST APIS

Q211. What Is REST?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

REST (Representational State Transfer) is an architectural style for building web services.

Principles

  • Stateless
  • Resource Based
  • Uniform Interface
  • Client Server Separation
  • Cacheable
Q212. Why Are REST APIs Stateless?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Answer

Each request contains all information required for processing. The server does not maintain client state between requests.

Benefit

  • Scalability
  • Load Balancing
  • Reliability
Q213. Common HTTP Methods

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Method Purpose
GET Read
POST Create
PUT Update
PATCH Partial Update
DELETE Delete
Q214. PUT vs PATCH

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

PUT PATCH
Full Update Partial Update
Replace Resource Modify Resource

Example

PUT typically sends the entire object. PATCH sends only changed fields.

Q215. What Is @RestController?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer


@RestController

=

@Controller

+

@ResponseBody

Returns JSON/XML responses directly.

Q216. Difference Between @Controller And @RestController

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

@Controller @RestController
Returns View Returns JSON
MVC Apps REST APIs
Q217. What Is ResponseEntity?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer

ResponseEntity provides full control over HTTP responses.

Can Control

  • Status Code
  • Headers
  • Response Body

Example


ResponseEntity.ok(user)
Q218. Common HTTP Status Codes

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Code Meaning
200 Success
201 Created
204 No Content
400 Bad Request
401 Unauthorized
403 Forbidden
404 Not Found
500 Internal Server Error

SECTION 38 - REQUEST MAPPING ANNOTATIONS

Q219. Difference Between @RequestParam And @PathVariable

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Example


/users/1

@PathVariable



/users?page=1

@RequestParam
Q220. What Is @RequestBody?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Maps HTTP request body to a Java object.

Example


POST /users

{
  "name":"John"
}

Automatically converted into DTO.

Q221. What Is @ResponseBody?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer

Converts Java objects into HTTP responses. Usually JSON.

SECTION 39 - VALIDATION

Q222. Why Validation Is Important?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Answer

Validation prevents invalid data from entering the system.

Benefits

  • Data Integrity
  • Security
  • Better User Experience
Q223. What Is @Valid?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Triggers Bean Validation before controller execution.

Example


@PostMapping

public User create(
  @Valid UserRequest request)
Q224. Common Validation Annotations

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High 


@NotNull

@NotBlank

@Size

@Email

@Min

@Max

@Pattern
Q225. @NotNull vs @NotEmpty vs @NotBlank

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Annotation Meaning
@NotNull Not Null
@NotEmpty Not Null And Size > 0
@NotBlank Not Null And Not Whitespace

SECTION 40 - EXCEPTION HANDLING

Q226. How Do You Handle Exceptions In Spring Boot?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Strong Answer

Use centralized exception handling through: 


@ControllerAdvice

instead of try-catch blocks everywhere.

Q227. What Is @ControllerAdvice?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Global exception handler for controllers.

Benefits

  • Centralized Logic
  • Consistent Responses
  • Cleaner Controllers
Q228. What Is @ExceptionHandler?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Answer

Handles specific exception types.

Example


@ExceptionHandler(
UserNotFoundException.class)
Q229. What Should A Good Error Response Contain?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Interview Ready Answer


timestamp

status

errorCode

message

path

correlationId

Production Insight

Correlation IDs significantly improve troubleshooting.

Q230. Senior Follow-Up: Why Avoid Generic Exception Catching?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Answer

Catching generic Exception:

  • Hides Root Cause
  • Makes Debugging Hard
  • Can Mask Serious Issues
  • Produces Poor Error Handling

Prefer specific exception types.

PART 10 COMPLETE

Next Part: Q231 - Q280 Topics: * Spring Data JPA * Hibernate Internals * Entity Lifecycle * Lazy vs Eager Loading * N+1 Problem * Fetch Strategies * Transactions * Locking * Optimistic Locking * Pessimistic Locking This is one of the most heavily asked sections for backend engineers.

SECTION 41 - SPRING DATA JPA

This is one of the highest ROI sections in Java backend interviews. A large percentage of production issues in Spring Boot systems originate from: * Incorrect Entity Design * N+1 Problems * Bad Transactions * Fetch Strategy Issues * Missing Indexes Senior interviewers know this and therefore spend significant time here.

Q231. What Is JPA?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

JPA (Java Persistence API) is a specification for object-relational mapping in Java. It defines how Java objects should be mapped to database tables.

Important

JPA is only a specification. It is not an implementation.

Q232. What Is Hibernate?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Hibernate is the most popular implementation of JPA.

Relationship


JPA

↓

Specification

↓

Hibernate

Implementation
Q233. Spring Data JPA vs Hibernate

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Spring Data JPA Hibernate
Abstraction Layer ORM Engine
Repository Support Persistence Provider
Built On JPA Implements JPA
Q234. What Is ORM?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

ORM (Object Relational Mapping) maps Java objects to relational database tables.

Example


User Object

↓

users Table
Q235. What Is An Entity?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Answer

An entity represents a database table.

Annotation


@Entity
Q236. Difference Between @Entity And @Table

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

@Entity @Table
Marks Entity Specifies Table Details

Example


@Entity
@Table(name="users")
Q237. What Is @Id?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Answer

Identifies primary key field.

Q238. @GeneratedValue Strategies

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Strategy Description
AUTO Provider Decides
IDENTITY Auto Increment
SEQUENCE Database Sequence
TABLE Table Generator

Production Insight

PostgreSQL systems commonly use SEQUENCE. MySQL commonly uses IDENTITY.

SECTION 42 - ENTITY LIFECYCLE

Q239. Entity Lifecycle States

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer


Transient

↓

Persistent

↓

Detached

↓

Removed
Q240. Explain Transient State

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Object exists in memory but not managed by Hibernate. It has no association with a persistence context and no corresponding row in the database until it is persisted.

Q241. Explain Persistent State

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Entity is managed by persistence context. Changes are tracked automatically.

Q242. What Is Persistence Context?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Persistence Context is Hibernate's first-level cache containing managed entities.

Benefit

Avoids repeated database queries for the same entity.

Q243. What Is Dirty Checking?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer

Hibernate automatically detects changes in managed entities and generates update statements during transaction commit.

Example


user.setName("Buddy");

commit()

↓

UPDATE users

SECTION 43 - ENTITY RELATIONSHIPS

Q244. JPA Relationship Types

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

  • @OneToOne
  • @OneToMany
  • @ManyToOne
  • @ManyToMany
Q245. Most Common Relationship?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Answer


@ManyToOne

For example: 


Job

↓

Recruiter

Many jobs belong to one recruiter.

Q246. mappedBy Meaning

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Interview Ready Answer

mappedBy identifies the owning side of a bidirectional relationship.

Prevents duplicate join table creation.

Q247. Cascade Types

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

  • PERSIST
  • MERGE
  • REMOVE
  • REFRESH
  • DETACH
  • ALL
Q248. What Is CascadeType.ALL?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

Applies all cascade operations.

Danger

May accidentally delete child records. Use carefully.

SECTION 44 - FETCHING STRATEGIES

Q249. Lazy Loading vs Eager Loading

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Lazy Eager
Load When Needed Load Immediately
Better Performance Can Overfetch
Q250. Default Fetch Types

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High

Relationship Default
@ManyToOne EAGER
@OneToOne EAGER
@OneToMany LAZY
@ManyToMany LAZY

Senior Advice

In production systems, many teams explicitly configure fetch types rather than relying on defaults.

Q251. What Is LazyInitializationException?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Occurs when a lazy-loaded relationship is accessed after the Hibernate session has closed.

Example


User Loaded

↓

Session Closed

↓

user.getOrders()

↓

Exception
Q252. How To Solve LazyInitializationException?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Senior Favorite

Good Solutions

  • Fetch Join
  • DTO Projection
  • Entity Graph
  • Transactional Boundary

Bad Solution


spring.jpa.open-in-view=true

Interview Trap

Do not present this as your primary solution in interviews.

Q253. What Is N+1 Query Problem?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

One query fetches parent entities. Additional queries fetch child entities individually.

Example


1 Query For Jobs

+

100 Queries For Recruiters

=

101 Queries
Q254. How To Detect N+1 Problem?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: High

  • Hibernate Logs
  • SQL Monitoring
  • APM Tools
  • Performance Testing
Q255. How To Fix N+1 Problem?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

  • Fetch Join
  • Entity Graph
  • DTO Projection
  • Batch Fetching

SECTION 45 - TRANSACTIONS

Q256. What Is A Transaction?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

A transaction is a unit of work executed completely or not at all.

Q257. ACID Properties

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Atomicity
  • Consistency
  • Isolation
  • Durability
Q258. What Is @Transactional?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Declares transactional boundaries. Spring manages commit and rollback automatically.

Q259. When Does Transaction Rollback?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

By default:

  • RuntimeException → Rollback
  • Error → Rollback
  • Checked Exception → No Rollback
Q260. Senior Interview Trap: Why Did Transaction Not Rollback?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Answer

  • Checked Exception
  • Self Invocation
  • Wrong Transaction Boundary
  • Proxy Bypass
  • No Active Transaction

PART 11 COMPLETE

Next Part: Q261 - Q320 Topics: * Transaction Propagation * Isolation Levels * Optimistic Locking * Pessimistic Locking * Query Optimization * Native Queries * JPQL * Specifications * Pagination * Auditing * Caching This is one of the most difficult and most frequently asked Spring Data interview sections.

SECTION 46 - TRANSACTION PROPAGATION

This is one of the most commonly misunderstood Spring topics. Many developers use @Transactional daily but cannot explain propagation behavior. Senior interviewers love these questions.

Q261. What Is Transaction Propagation?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Propagation determines how a transactional method behaves when called from another transactional method.

Example


Service A

↓

Service B

Should Service B join the existing transaction or create a new one? Propagation defines that behavior.

Q262. Propagation Types

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Extremely High 


REQUIRED

REQUIRES_NEW

SUPPORTS

MANDATORY

NOT_SUPPORTED

NEVER

NESTED
Q263. REQUIRED Propagation

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Default propagation mode.

Behavior

  • Join Existing Transaction
  • Create New Transaction If None Exists

Production Usage

Most commonly used propagation mode.

Q264. REQUIRES_NEW

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Always creates a new transaction.

Behavior


Existing Transaction

↓

Suspended

↓

New Transaction Starts

Use Case

Audit Logging.

Even if the main transaction fails, audit logs can still commit.

Q265. SUPPORTS Propagation

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Uses existing transaction if available. Otherwise executes without a transaction.

Q266. MANDATORY Propagation

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Requires an existing transaction. Throws exception if none exists.

Q267. Senior Scenario: Order Created Successfully But Audit Record Missing. How Would You Design It?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

Use: 


@Transactional

Order Creation

and 


@Transactional(
propagation = REQUIRES_NEW)

Audit Service

to isolate audit transaction behavior.

SECTION 47 - TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVELS

Q268. Why Isolation Levels Exist?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Multiple transactions may access the same data concurrently. Isolation levels control visibility of uncommitted changes.

Q269. Common Concurrency Problems

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Dirty Read
  • Non Repeatable Read
  • Phantom Read
Q270. What Is Dirty Read?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Transaction A reads data modified by Transaction B before Transaction B commits.

If Transaction B rolls back, Transaction A read invalid data.

Q271. What Is Non-Repeatable Read?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Reading the same row twice returns different values because another transaction updated it.

Q272. What Is Phantom Read?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Repeated query returns different numbers of rows because another transaction inserted or deleted records.

Q273. Isolation Levels

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Level Protection
READ_UNCOMMITTED Lowest
READ_COMMITTED Dirty Read Protected
REPEATABLE_READ Non Repeatable Read Protected
SERIALIZABLE Highest Protection
Q274. Most Common Isolation Level?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

READ_COMMITTED

Common default in PostgreSQL.

SECTION 48 - LOCKING

Q275. Why Locking Is Required?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

To prevent concurrent updates from causing data inconsistencies.

Q276. Optimistic Locking

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Assumes conflicts are rare. Detects conflicts during update.

Annotation


@Version
Q277. Optimistic Locking Example

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


Version = 5

User A Reads

User B Reads

User A Updates

Version = 6

User B Updates

↓

OptimisticLockException
Q278. Pessimistic Locking

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Locks the record immediately. Other transactions must wait.

Q279. Optimistic vs Pessimistic Locking

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Optimistic Pessimistic
No Immediate Lock Immediate Lock
Higher Throughput Lower Throughput
Conflict Detection Conflict Prevention

Interview Gold Line

Most business applications prefer optimistic locking because conflicts are relatively rare.

Q280. Banking Transfer Scenario. Which Locking Strategy?

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Strong Answer

For critical monetary operations:

  • Pessimistic Locking
  • Serializable Isolation

may be appropriate depending on requirements.

SECTION 49 - JPQL & QUERIES

Q281. What Is JPQL?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

JPQL operates on entities rather than database tables.

SQL


SELECT * FROM users

JPQL


SELECT u FROM User u
Q282. JPQL vs Native Query

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

JPQL Native SQL
Database Independent Database Specific
Entity Based Table Based
Portable More Powerful
Q283. When Would You Use Native Query?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • Complex Reporting
  • Window Functions
  • Database-Specific Features
  • Performance Optimization
Q284. What Is DTO Projection?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Fetch only required fields instead of loading entire entities.

Benefit

  • Better Performance
  • Less Memory Usage
  • Avoid Lazy Loading Issues
Q285. Why Avoid Returning Entities Directly?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

  • Security Risk
  • Lazy Loading Problems
  • API Contract Instability
  • Overfetching

Prefer DTOs.

SECTION 50 - PAGINATION & SORTING

Q286. Why Pagination?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Loading millions of rows at once is expensive. Pagination improves:

  • Performance
  • Memory Usage
  • User Experience
Q287. Pageable Interface

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Spring Data abstraction for pagination.

Q288. Page vs Slice

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Page Slice
Total Count Query No Count Query
More Information Better Performance
Q289. Why Can Pagination Become Slow?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • Large OFFSET Values
  • Missing Indexes
  • Heavy Sorting
  • Complex Joins
Q290. Keyset Pagination

ROI: Architect Level ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Uses the last seen record rather than OFFSET.

Benefit

Much faster for large datasets.

Example


WHERE id > lastSeenId

PART 12 COMPLETE

Current Progress: Q1 - Q290 Covered: * Java Core * JVM * Collections * Concurrency * Java 8 * OOP * SOLID * Spring Core * Spring Boot * Spring MVC * Validation * Exception Handling * JPA * Hibernate * Transactions * Locking * Query Optimization * Pagination Next Section (Q291-Q360): * Hibernate Performance Tuning * Caching * Auditing * Specifications * Criteria API * Batch Processing * Spring Cache * Redis Integration * Production Performance Scenarios This section contains many real-world questions that senior interviewers ask after basic JPA concepts.

SECTION 51 - HIBERNATE PERFORMANCE TUNING

This section is extremely important for Senior Software Engineer interviews. Most real-world Spring Boot performance issues are caused by: * N+1 Queries * Missing Indexes * Excessive Fetching * Inefficient Pagination * Poor Query Design Senior interviewers often use these questions to separate developers who can build applications from engineers who can operate them at scale.

Q291. What Are The Most Common Hibernate Performance Problems?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

  • N+1 Queries
  • Missing Database Indexes
  • Large Object Graphs
  • Excessive Eager Loading
  • Slow Native Queries
  • Inefficient Pagination
  • Large Transactions
Q292. How Would You Diagnose Slow Hibernate Queries?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  1. Enable SQL Logging
  2. Review Execution Plans
  3. Check Index Usage
  4. Analyze N+1 Queries
  5. Measure Query Time
  6. Use APM Tools

Tools


New Relic

Datadog

Elastic APM

Grafana
Q293. What Is Fetch Join?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Fetch Join loads related entities in a single query.

Benefit

Eliminates N+1 query problems.

Example


SELECT u
FROM User u
JOIN FETCH u.orders
Q294. What Is Batch Fetching?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Hibernate loads related entities in groups rather than one at a time.

Benefit

Reduces database round trips.

Q295. What Is Open Session In View (OSIV)?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

OSIV keeps the Hibernate session open during web request processing.

Benefit

Prevents LazyInitializationException.

Problem

  • Hidden Queries
  • Performance Issues
  • Unexpected Database Access

Interview Gold Line

Modern production systems often disable OSIV and use DTO projections or fetch joins.

Q296. Why Avoid Returning Large Entity Graphs?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

  • Slow Serialization
  • Large Responses
  • Memory Usage
  • Recursive Relationships
  • N+1 Risks

SECTION 52 - DATABASE INDEXING

Q297. What Is A Database Index?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

An index is a data structure that improves query performance by reducing the amount of data scanned.

Analogy

Like a book index. Without it, every page must be searched.

Q298. Benefits Of Indexes

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

  • Faster Reads
  • Efficient Sorting
  • Faster Filtering
  • Faster Joins
Q299. Drawbacks Of Indexes

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

  • Additional Storage
  • Slower Inserts
  • Slower Updates
  • Maintenance Cost
Q300. How Do You Identify Missing Indexes?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • EXPLAIN ANALYZE
  • Execution Plans
  • Slow Query Logs
  • Database Monitoring
Q301. What Columns Should Typically Be Indexed?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Primary Keys
  • Foreign Keys
  • Search Columns
  • Join Columns
  • Frequently Filtered Fields

SECTION 53 - HIBERNATE CACHING

Q302. What Is Hibernate First-Level Cache?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

The persistence context acts as Hibernate's first-level cache.

Scope

Per Session.

Important

Always enabled.

Q303. First-Level Cache Example

ROI: High ★★★★☆ 


findById(1)

↓

Database Query

findById(1)

↓

Cache Hit
Q304. What Is Second-Level Cache?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Second-level cache is shared across sessions.

Examples

  • Ehcache
  • Hazelcast
  • Redis
Q305. First-Level vs Second-Level Cache

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

First Level Second Level
Per Session Shared
Always Enabled Optional
Short Lived Longer Lived

SECTION 54 - SPRING CACHE

Q306. What Is Spring Cache?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Spring Cache provides declarative caching support using annotations.

Benefits

  • Reduced Database Load
  • Improved Response Time
  • Better Scalability
Q307. What Is @Cacheable?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Caches method results.

Flow


Request

↓

Cache Check

↓

Cache Hit?

↓

Return

↓

Else Execute Method
Q308. What Is @CacheEvict?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Removes entries from cache.

Example


Update User

↓

Evict Cache
Q309. What Is @CachePut?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Updates cache without skipping method execution.

Q310. When Should You Not Cache?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • Highly Dynamic Data
  • Sensitive Data
  • Large Objects
  • Rapidly Changing Records

SECTION 55 - REDIS

Q311. What Is Redis?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Redis is an in-memory data store commonly used for:

  • Caching
  • Sessions
  • Rate Limiting
  • Distributed Locks
  • Pub/Sub
Q312. Why Is Redis Fast?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Because data is stored primarily in memory.

Q313. Redis Use Cases In Spring Boot

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Cache Product Data
  • Store User Sessions
  • OTP Storage
  • API Rate Limiting
  • Distributed Locking
Q314. Cache Aside Pattern

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer


Request

↓

Cache

↓

Miss

↓

Database

↓

Store In Cache

↓

Return

Most Common Caching Pattern

Used in many Spring Boot applications.

Q315. Cache Stampede

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Many requests simultaneously miss cache and hit the database.

Solutions

  • Distributed Locking
  • Prewarming
  • Randomized TTL
  • Request Coalescing
Q316. Cache Penetration

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Answer

Repeated requests for non-existent data bypass cache and hit database repeatedly.

Solutions

  • Bloom Filters
  • Negative Caching
Q317. Cache Eviction Strategies

ROI: High ★★★★☆

  • LRU
  • LFU
  • TTL Based
  • Manual Eviction
Q318. Redis vs Database

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Redis Database
In Memory Disk Based
Very Fast Slower
Cache Layer Source Of Truth
Q319. Production Scenario: Database CPU Suddenly Spikes. What Would You Check?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Senior Answer

  • Cache Hit Ratio
  • N+1 Queries
  • Slow Queries
  • Missing Indexes
  • Traffic Spike
  • Recent Deployment
  • Connection Pool Usage
Q320. Production Scenario: Redis Goes Down. What Happens?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

Depends on architecture.

  • Cache Misses Increase
  • Database Load Increases
  • Latency Increases
  • Potential Database Overload

Mitigation

  • Redis Cluster
  • Fallback Logic
  • Circuit Breakers
  • Graceful Degradation

PART 13 COMPLETE

Current Progress: Q1 - Q320 Next Section (Q321-Q400) Topics: * Spring Security * Authentication * Authorization * JWT * OAuth2 * Filters * SecurityContext * CSRF * CORS * Security Best Practices This is one of the most commonly asked sections for modern Spring Boot backend interviews and is highly relevant to your experience with JWT-based authentication systems.

SECTION 56 - SPRING SECURITY FUNDAMENTALS

For a 4+ year Spring Boot engineer, Spring Security is one of the highest ROI interview topics. Since you've worked with JWT authentication, expect deep follow-up questions. Interviewers frequently move from: "What is JWT?" to "Explain exactly how authentication happens in your application."

Q321. What Is Spring Security?

ROI: ★★★★★ Frequency: Guaranteed

Interview Ready Answer

Spring Security is a framework that provides:

  • Authentication
  • Authorization
  • Session Management
  • CSRF Protection
  • Password Encoding
  • Security Filters
Q322. Authentication vs Authorization

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Authentication Authorization
Who Are You? What Can You Do?
Identity Verification Permission Check

Example

Login verifies identity. Role check verifies permissions.

Q323. What Is Principal?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Represents the currently authenticated user.

Q324. What Is SecurityContext?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

SecurityContext stores authentication information for the current request.

Contains

  • User Details
  • Authorities
  • Authentication Status
Q325. What Is Authentication Object?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Represents authentication state.

Contains

  • Principal
  • Credentials
  • Authorities

SECTION 57 - SPRING SECURITY FILTER CHAIN

Q326. What Is Spring Security Filter Chain?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Spring Security intercepts requests using a chain of filters.

Flow


Request

↓

Security Filters

↓

Authentication

↓

Authorization

↓

Controller
Q327. Why Filters?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Filters allow security checks before requests reach controllers.

Q328. OncePerRequestFilter

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Ensures a filter executes only once per request.

Common Usage


JWT Filter
Q329. Explain JWT Filter Flow

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer


Request

↓

Authorization Header

↓

Extract JWT

↓

Validate JWT

↓

Load User

↓

Create Authentication

↓

Store In SecurityContext

↓

Continue Request

This is one of the most important answers for JWT-based applications.

Q330. What Happens If JWT Is Invalid?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Authentication is not established. Request receives: 


401 Unauthorized

SECTION 58 - JWT (JSON WEB TOKEN)

Q331. What Is JWT?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

JWT is a compact, self-contained token used for authentication and authorization.

Q332. JWT Structure

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer


Header

.

Payload

.

Signature

Example


xxxxx.yyyyy.zzzzz
Q333. JWT Header Contains?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer


Algorithm

Token Type
Q334. JWT Payload Contains?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

  • User Id
  • Username
  • Roles
  • Claims
  • Expiration
Q335. JWT Signature Purpose

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Ensures token integrity.

If payload changes, signature validation fails.

Q336. Why JWT Is Stateless?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Server does not need to store session data. All required information exists inside the token.

Q337. JWT Advantages

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

  • Stateless
  • Scalable
  • Microservice Friendly
  • No Session Storage
Q338. JWT Disadvantages

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Revocation Complexity
  • Larger Payloads
  • Expiration Management
  • Security Risks If Misused
Q339. Where Should JWT Be Stored?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • HttpOnly Cookie (Preferred)
  • Secure Cookie

Avoid


localStorage

for highly sensitive applications because of XSS concerns.

Q340. How Do You Logout With JWT?

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

JWT is stateless. Logout requires additional mechanisms.

Options

  • Token Blacklist
  • Short Expiration
  • Refresh Token Rotation

SECTION 59 - PASSWORD SECURITY

Q341. Why Not Store Plain Passwords?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Database compromise would expose all user credentials.

Q342. What Is Password Hashing?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Transforms passwords into irreversible hashes.

Q343. Why BCrypt?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

  • Salted
  • Adaptive
  • Resistant To Brute Force
Q344. What Is Salt?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Random value added before hashing.

Purpose

Prevents rainbow table attacks.

SECTION 60 - CORS & CSRF

Q345. What Is CORS?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Cross-Origin Resource Sharing controls which domains may access APIs.

Q346. Why CORS Exists?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Browsers enforce same-origin policy for security.

Q347. What Is CSRF?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Cross-Site Request Forgery tricks authenticated users into executing unintended actions.

Q348. Why Is CSRF Less Relevant For JWT APIs?

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Strong Answer

When JWT is sent in Authorization headers rather than cookies, browsers do not automatically attach credentials. Therefore CSRF risk is significantly reduced.

Q349. Security Best Practices

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • HTTPS Everywhere
  • Strong Password Hashing
  • Least Privilege
  • Input Validation
  • Secure Headers
  • Token Expiration
  • Audit Logging
Q350. Production Scenario: Users Suddenly Receive 401 Errors. How Would You Investigate?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Senior Answer

  1. Check JWT Expiration
  2. Check Secret Key Changes
  3. Check Security Filter Logs
  4. Verify Authorization Header
  5. Check Clock Synchronization
  6. Review Recent Deployments
  7. Verify User Status

PART 14 COMPLETE

Current Progress: Q1 - Q350 Completed: * Java Core * JVM * Concurrency * Java 8 * OOP * SOLID * Design Patterns * Spring Core * Spring Boot * Spring MVC * Validation * Exception Handling * JPA/Hibernate * Transactions * Caching * Redis * Spring Security * JWT Next Section (Q351-Q450): * Microservices * Service Discovery * API Gateway * Feign Client * Resilience4j * Circuit Breaker * Distributed Transactions * Saga Pattern * Event Driven Architecture * Kafka * RabbitMQ This is where many Senior Software Engineer interviews become architecture-focused.

SECTION 61 - MICROSERVICES FUNDAMENTALS

This section is one of the most important for Senior Software Engineer interviews. Many candidates can build CRUD APIs. Far fewer can explain: * Why Microservices? * When Not To Use Them? * Distributed System Challenges? * Production Tradeoffs? Senior interviewers focus heavily on these areas.

Q351. What Are Microservices?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Microservices are an architectural style where an application is split into small, independently deployable services. Each service:

  • Owns A Business Capability
  • Can Be Developed Independently
  • Can Be Deployed Independently
  • Can Scale Independently
Q352. Monolith vs Microservices

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Monolith Microservices
Single Deployment Multiple Deployments
Single Codebase Distributed Codebases
Simpler Operations Complex Operations
Easier To Start Better Scalability
Q353. Advantages Of Microservices

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

  • Independent Deployment
  • Independent Scaling
  • Technology Flexibility
  • Fault Isolation
  • Team Autonomy
Q354. Challenges Of Microservices

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

  • Distributed Transactions
  • Network Failures
  • Data Consistency
  • Monitoring Complexity
  • Deployment Complexity
  • Service Discovery
  • Security

Interview Gold Line

Microservices trade development flexibility for operational complexity.

Q355. When Would You Avoid Microservices?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • Small Team
  • Simple Product
  • Early Startup
  • Limited Infrastructure

A modular monolith is often a better starting point.

Q356. Database Per Service Pattern

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Each microservice owns its database. Other services cannot directly access it.

Benefit

  • Loose Coupling
  • Independent Evolution
  • Service Ownership
Q357. Why Not Share Database Across Services?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

  • Tight Coupling
  • Schema Dependencies
  • Deployment Risks
  • Reduced Autonomy

SECTION 62 - SERVICE DISCOVERY

Q358. Why Service Discovery?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Microservice instances frequently change. Hardcoding IP addresses is impractical.

Service Discovery Solves

  • Dynamic Registration
  • Dynamic Lookup
  • Load Balancing
Q359. What Is Eureka?

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Netflix Eureka is a service registry. Services register themselves and discover other services dynamically.

Q360. Service Discovery Flow

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


User Service

↓

Register

↓

Eureka

↓

Order Service

↓

Lookup User Service

SECTION 63 - API GATEWAY

Q361. What Is API Gateway?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

API Gateway is the single entry point for client requests.

Responsibilities

  • Routing
  • Authentication
  • Rate Limiting
  • Logging
  • Monitoring
Q362. Why API Gateway?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Without Gateway: 


Client

↓

User Service

Order Service

Payment Service

Inventory Service

Gateway centralizes access.

Q363. Spring Cloud Gateway

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Interview Ready Answer

Spring's modern API Gateway solution.

Supports

  • Routing
  • Filters
  • Authentication
  • Load Balancing
Q364. API Gateway Benefits

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Centralized Security
  • Centralized Logging
  • Simplified Clients
  • Traffic Control
Q365. Gateway vs Load Balancer

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Gateway Load Balancer
Application Layer Traffic Distribution
Routing + Security Primarily Routing

SECTION 64 - INTER-SERVICE COMMUNICATION

Q366. How Do Microservices Communicate?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

  • REST APIs
  • gRPC
  • Kafka
  • RabbitMQ
  • Events
Q367. Synchronous vs Asynchronous Communication

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Synchronous Asynchronous
Waits For Response No Immediate Response
REST Kafka
Tighter Coupling Looser Coupling
Q368. What Is OpenFeign?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Feign is a declarative HTTP client.

Instead Of


RestTemplate

WebClient

Manual HTTP Calls

Developers define interfaces.

Q369. Feign Advantages

ROI: High ★★★★☆

  • Less Boilerplate
  • Readable Code
  • Easy Integration
  • Service Discovery Support
Q370. Feign vs RestTemplate

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Feign RestTemplate
Declarative Imperative
Less Boilerplate More Code
Microservice Friendly Legacy Approach
Q371. Feign vs WebClient

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Feign WebClient
Declarative Reactive
Simpler More Powerful
Blocking Usually Non Blocking
Q372. What Happens If Service B Is Down?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Senior Answer

Without resilience mechanisms:

  • Timeouts
  • Failures
  • Cascading Failures

Solutions:

  • Circuit Breaker
  • Retry
  • Fallback
  • Timeouts
Q373. Why Timeouts Matter?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Without timeouts, threads may remain blocked indefinitely waiting for downstream services.

Result

  • Thread Pool Exhaustion
  • Slow APIs
  • System Instability
Q374. Cascading Failure

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Failure in one service causes failures in dependent services.

Example


Payment Service Down

↓

Order Service Waiting

↓

Gateway Waiting

↓

Entire System Slows
Q375. Production Scenario: One Service Response Time Jumps From 100ms To 10 Seconds. What Happens?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Senior Answer

  • Request Queues Build Up
  • Threads Become Blocked
  • Timeouts Increase
  • Circuit Breakers May Trigger
  • Dependent Services Slow Down
  • Cascading Failure Risk

PART 15 COMPLETE

Current Progress: Q1 - Q375 Next Section (Q376 - Q450) Topics: * Resilience4j * Circuit Breaker * Retry * Bulkhead * Rate Limiter * Distributed Transactions * Saga Pattern * Event Driven Architecture * Kafka * RabbitMQ This is one of the highest ROI architecture sections for Senior Software Engineer interviews.

SECTION 65 - RESILIENCE PATTERNS

At Senior Software Engineer level, interviewers expect you to understand what happens when services fail. In distributed systems: Network failures are normal. Service failures are normal. Timeouts are normal. The real question is: "How does your system survive them?"

Q376. What Is Resilience4j?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Resilience4j is a fault-tolerance library for Java applications.

Main Features

  • Circuit Breaker
  • Retry
  • Rate Limiter
  • Bulkhead
  • Time Limiter
Q377. Why Do We Need Resilience Patterns?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Because failures are unavoidable in distributed systems.

Without Protection

  • Cascading Failures
  • Slow Responses
  • System Outages

SECTION 66 - CIRCUIT BREAKER

Q378. What Is Circuit Breaker?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Circuit Breaker prevents repeated calls to a failing service.

Analogy

Like an electrical circuit breaker. When failures increase, requests stop flowing temporarily.

Q379. Why Circuit Breaker?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

  • Prevent Cascading Failures
  • Reduce Resource Consumption
  • Improve Stability
  • Fast Failure Response
Q380. Circuit Breaker States

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


CLOSED

↓

OPEN

↓

HALF_OPEN
Q381. Explain CLOSED State

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Requests flow normally. Failures are monitored.

Q382. Explain OPEN State

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Requests are blocked immediately. Fallback response is returned.

Q383. Explain HALF_OPEN State

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Limited requests are allowed to test recovery.

If Successful


HALF_OPEN

↓

CLOSED
Q384. Circuit Breaker Example

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


Order Service

↓

Payment Service

↓

Repeated Failures

↓

Circuit Opens

↓

Fallback Response
Q385. What Is Fallback?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Alternative logic executed when primary service fails.

Examples

  • Cached Data
  • Default Response
  • Graceful Error Message
Q386. Why Not Retry Forever?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Infinite retries can overwhelm failing services.

Retries must be limited.

SECTION 67 - RETRY PATTERN

Q387. What Is Retry Pattern?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Automatically retries failed operations.

Useful For

  • Temporary Network Failures
  • Transient Errors
  • Short Service Outages
Q388. When Should Retry Be Avoided?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

  • Permanent Failures
  • Validation Errors
  • Bad Requests
  • Authorization Errors
Q389. Exponential Backoff

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Delay increases between retries.

Example


1 sec

2 sec

4 sec

8 sec

Benefit

Reduces pressure on recovering systems.

SECTION 68 - BULKHEAD PATTERN

Q390. What Is Bulkhead Pattern?

ROI: Architect Favorite ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Bulkhead isolates resources so failure in one component does not affect others.

Example

Separate thread pools for different downstream services.

Q391. Why Bulkhead?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Prevent resource exhaustion from spreading across the entire application.

SECTION 69 - RATE LIMITING

Q392. What Is Rate Limiting?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Restricts the number of requests allowed during a period.

Q393. Why Rate Limiting?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Prevent Abuse
  • Protect Services
  • Improve Stability
  • Prevent DDoS Effects
Q394. Common Rate Limiting Algorithms

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

  • Token Bucket
  • Leaky Bucket
  • Fixed Window
  • Sliding Window

SECTION 70 - DISTRIBUTED TRANSACTIONS

Q395. Why Distributed Transactions Are Difficult?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Multiple services own different databases. A single local transaction cannot span all services.

Q396. Example Distributed Transaction

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


Order Service

↓

Payment Service

↓

Inventory Service

All three must remain consistent.

Q397. What Is Two-Phase Commit (2PC)?

ROI: Architect Level ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Coordinator asks all participants: 


Can You Commit?

If everyone agrees: 


Commit

Otherwise: 


Rollback
Q398. Problems With 2PC

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Blocking
  • Complexity
  • Scalability Issues
  • Single Coordinator Dependency

SECTION 71 - SAGA PATTERN

Q399. What Is Saga Pattern?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Saga manages distributed transactions through a sequence of local transactions.

If one step fails, compensating actions undo previous work.

Q400. Saga Example

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


Create Order

↓

Reserve Inventory

↓

Charge Payment

↓

Ship Order

If Payment Fails


Refund

Cancel Reservation

Cancel Order

Interview Gold Line

Modern microservice systems generally prefer Saga over Two-Phase Commit because it scales better and avoids distributed locking.

PART 16 COMPLETE

Current Progress: Q1 - Q400 Topics Completed: * Java Core * JVM * Concurrency * Spring Core * Spring Boot * Spring MVC * Validation * Exception Handling * JPA/Hibernate * Transactions * Redis * Spring Security * JWT * Microservices * Service Discovery * API Gateway * Feign * Resilience4j * Circuit Breakers * Retry * Bulkhead * Rate Limiting * Distributed Transactions * Saga Pattern Next Part (Q401-Q500): * Kafka * RabbitMQ * Event Driven Architecture * Event Sourcing * CQRS * Outbox Pattern * Idempotency * Exactly Once Processing * Distributed System Design Scenarios This is one of the most valuable sections for senior backend and microservices interviews.

SECTION 72 - EVENT DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE (EDA)

This is one of the most important topics in modern microservices interviews. Many large-scale systems avoid synchronous REST communication wherever possible and rely heavily on events. Examples: * Amazon * Netflix * Uber * Airbnb * LinkedIn

Q401. What Is Event Driven Architecture?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Event Driven Architecture (EDA) is a design pattern where services communicate through events instead of direct synchronous calls.

Example


Order Created

↓

Publish Event

↓

Inventory Service

Payment Service

Notification Service
Q402. Why Event Driven Architecture?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

  • Loose Coupling
  • Scalability
  • Fault Isolation
  • Asynchronous Processing
Q403. Event Driven vs REST Communication

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

REST Events
Synchronous Asynchronous
Tighter Coupling Loose Coupling
Immediate Response Eventually Consistent
Q404. Event Producer vs Consumer

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Producer Consumer
Publishes Event Processes Event
Q405. What Is Eventual Consistency?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Systems may temporarily have different states but eventually become consistent.

Important

This is a core concept in distributed systems.

SECTION 73 - APACHE KAFKA

Q406. What Is Kafka?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Kafka is a distributed event streaming platform used for:

  • Messaging
  • Event Streaming
  • Data Pipelines
  • Real-Time Processing
Q407. Why Kafka Is Popular?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • High Throughput
  • Scalability
  • Fault Tolerance
  • Durability
Q408. Core Kafka Components

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★ 


Producer

Topic

Partition

Broker

Consumer

Consumer Group
Q409. What Is A Topic?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

A topic is a logical category of events.

Examples


orders

payments

users
Q410. What Is A Partition?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Partitions allow topics to be distributed and processed in parallel.

Benefit

  • Scalability
  • Parallelism
Q411. Why Multiple Partitions?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Without partitions, a topic becomes a bottleneck.

Partitions allow multiple consumers to process events concurrently.

Q412. What Is A Kafka Broker?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

A Kafka server responsible for storing and serving messages.

Q413. What Is Replication?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Kafka replicates partitions across brokers for fault tolerance.

Q414. Leader And Follower Partitions

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Leader handles reads/writes. Followers replicate data.

Q415. What Is A Consumer Group?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

A group of consumers cooperating to process topic partitions.

Important Rule

One partition can be consumed by only one consumer in a group at a time.

Q416. Consumer Group Example

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


Topic

4 Partitions

↓

Consumer Group

2 Consumers

Each consumer receives some partitions.

Q417. What Is Offset?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Offset is the position of a message inside a partition.

Q418. Why Offsets Matter?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Offsets allow consumers to resume processing from a specific position.

Q419. What Happens If Consumer Crashes?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

Consumer restarts and continues from committed offset.

Q420. At Most Once Delivery

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Messages may be lost but never duplicated.

Q421. At Least Once Delivery

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Messages are never lost but duplicates may occur.

Most Common

Used by many production systems.

Q422. Exactly Once Delivery

ROI: Architect Level ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Message processed exactly one time.

More complex and expensive.

Q423. Why Is Idempotency Important?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Duplicate messages may occur. Processing should remain safe.

Example

Payment should not be charged twice.

Q424. What Is Idempotency?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Executing the same operation multiple times produces the same result.

SECTION 74 - RABBITMQ

Q425. What Is RabbitMQ?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

RabbitMQ is a message broker implementing AMQP.

Use Cases

  • Task Queues
  • Background Processing
  • Reliable Messaging
Q426. RabbitMQ Core Components

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★ 


Producer

Exchange

Queue

Consumer
Q427. Exchange Purpose

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Routes messages to queues.

Q428. Kafka vs RabbitMQ

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Kafka RabbitMQ
Event Streaming Message Broker
Very High Throughput Flexible Routing
Long Retention Queue Oriented
Q429. When Would You Choose Kafka?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Event Streaming
  • Analytics
  • High Throughput
  • Data Pipelines
Q430. When Would You Choose RabbitMQ?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Task Queues
  • Complex Routing
  • Work Distribution
  • Background Jobs

SECTION 75 - OUTBOX PATTERN

Q431. What Is The Dual Write Problem?

ROI: Architect Favorite ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer


Save To Database

+

Publish Event

One operation succeeds. Other fails. Data becomes inconsistent.

Q432. Outbox Pattern

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Store event in database outbox table within the same transaction. A separate process publishes events reliably.

Q433. Why Outbox Pattern?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Consistency
  • Reliability
  • Avoid Dual Writes
Q434. Production Scenario: Consumer Processes Same Event Twice. How Do You Prevent Duplicate Business Impact?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Answer

  • Idempotency Key
  • Unique Constraints
  • Processed Event Table
  • Deduplication Logic
Q435. Production Scenario: Kafka Broker Goes Down. What Happens?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

Depends on replication configuration.

  • Leader Election
  • Replica Promotion
  • Temporary Unavailability
  • Possible Data Loss If Misconfigured

PART 17 COMPLETE

Current Progress: Q1 - Q435 Next Section (Q436-Q520) Topics: * System Design * Scalability * Load Balancing * CAP Theorem * Database Scaling * Sharding * Replication * Distributed Caching * CDN * High Availability * Disaster Recovery These questions are increasingly common for Senior Software Engineer roles with 4+ years experience.

SECTION 76 - SYSTEM DESIGN FUNDAMENTALS

This section becomes extremely important for Senior Software Engineer interviews. Many companies may not ask you to fully design YouTube or Uber. However, they often ask: * How would you scale this? * What happens at 10x traffic? * What breaks first? * How would you redesign it? Interviewers are evaluating engineering thinking rather than memorized answers.

Q436. What Is Scalability?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Scalability is the ability of a system to handle increasing load without significant degradation.

Examples

  • More Users
  • More Requests
  • More Data
Q437. Vertical Scaling vs Horizontal Scaling

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Vertical Horizontal
Bigger Machine More Machines
Scale Up Scale Out
Limited Growth Highly Scalable

Interview Gold Line

Modern cloud systems primarily favor horizontal scaling.

Q438. Why Horizontal Scaling?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Higher Availability
  • Fault Tolerance
  • Better Scalability
  • Cloud Friendly
Q439. What Is Load Balancing?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Load balancing distributes traffic across multiple servers.

Goal

  • Prevent Overload
  • Improve Availability
  • Improve Throughput
Q440. Types Of Load Balancers

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Layer 4 Layer 7
TCP/UDP HTTP/HTTPS
Network Level Application Level
Q441. Common Load Balancing Algorithms

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Round Robin
  • Least Connections
  • Weighted Round Robin
  • IP Hash
Q442. Session Affinity (Sticky Sessions)

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Answer

Requests from a user are routed to the same server.

Problem

Can reduce load balancing effectiveness.

SECTION 77 - CAP THEOREM

Q443. What Is CAP Theorem?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

A distributed system can provide at most two of:

  • Consistency
  • Availability
  • Partition Tolerance
Q444. Explain Consistency

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Every node sees the same data.

Q445. Explain Availability

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Every request receives a response.

Q446. Explain Partition Tolerance

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

System continues functioning despite network failures between nodes.

Q447. Why Is Partition Tolerance Mandatory?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

Network failures are unavoidable in distributed systems. Therefore modern distributed systems must tolerate partitions.

Q448. CP vs AP Systems

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

CP AP
Consistency First Availability First
Banking Social Media

SECTION 78 - DATABASE SCALING

Q449. Database Scaling Strategies

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Indexing
  • Caching
  • Read Replicas
  • Sharding
  • Partitioning
Q450. What Are Read Replicas?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Copies of primary database used for read operations.

Benefit

Reduce load on primary database.

Q451. Primary-Replica Architecture

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★ 


Primary DB

↓

Replicate

↓

Replica 1

Replica 2

Replica 3
Q452. What Is Replication Lag?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Delay between primary update and replica synchronization.

Result

Stale Reads.

Q453. What Is Database Sharding?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Sharding splits data across multiple databases.

Example


Users 1-1M

↓

Shard A

Users 1M-2M

↓

Shard B
Q454. Why Sharding?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Scale Storage
  • Scale Throughput
  • Reduce Hotspots
Q455. Challenges Of Sharding

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Complex Queries
  • Cross-Shard Joins
  • Rebalancing
  • Operational Complexity
Q456. Horizontal Partitioning vs Sharding

ROI: Senior Favorite ★★★★★

Answer

Partitioning often occurs inside one database. Sharding distributes data across multiple databases.

SECTION 79 - CACHING AT SCALE

Q457. Why Caching?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

  • Reduce Latency
  • Reduce Database Load
  • Improve Throughput
Q458. Levels Of Caching

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Browser Cache
  • CDN Cache
  • Application Cache
  • Database Cache
Q459. What Is CDN?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Content Delivery Network stores content closer to users.

Benefits

  • Lower Latency
  • Reduced Origin Load
  • Global Distribution
Q460. Example CDN Usage

ROI: High ★★★★☆

  • Images
  • Videos
  • JavaScript
  • CSS

SECTION 80 - HIGH AVAILABILITY

Q461. What Is High Availability?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

System remains operational despite failures.

Q462. High Availability Techniques

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Redundancy
  • Replication
  • Failover
  • Load Balancing
  • Multi AZ Deployment
Q463. What Is Failover?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Automatic switching to backup resources during failures.

Q464. What Is Disaster Recovery?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Processes and infrastructure used to recover from catastrophic failures.

Q465. RTO vs RPO

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

RTO RPO
Recovery Time Objective Recovery Point Objective
Downtime Allowed Data Loss Allowed

SECTION 81 - SYSTEM DESIGN SCENARIO QUESTIONS

Q466. How Would You Scale A Job Portal From 10K To 10 Million Users?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  1. Add Load Balancer
  2. Multiple Application Instances
  3. Redis Cache
  4. Database Read Replicas
  5. Asynchronous Processing
  6. Kafka Events
  7. CDN
  8. Monitoring
  9. Sharding If Necessary
Q467. API Suddenly Receives 100x Traffic. What Breaks First?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Answer

  • Connection Pool
  • Database
  • Thread Pool
  • Cache
  • Downstream Services
Q468. How Would You Investigate A Production Outage?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  1. Determine Scope
  2. Check Monitoring
  3. Check Recent Deployments
  4. Review Logs
  5. Review Metrics
  6. Review Dependencies
  7. Mitigate
  8. Root Cause Analysis
Q469. Production Troubleshooting Mindset

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Gold Answer

Always collect evidence before conclusions.

Use:

  • Metrics
  • Logs
  • Traces
  • Dashboards

Never rely on assumptions.

Q470. What Separates Senior Engineers From Mid-Level Engineers?

ROI: Architect Favorite ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • System Thinking
  • Production Ownership
  • Scalability Awareness
  • Incident Handling
  • Tradeoff Analysis
  • Mentorship

SECTION 82 - AWS FOR SPRING BOOT ENGINEERS

For modern backend interviews, especially for Spring Boot and Microservices roles, AWS questions are extremely common. Interviewers often don't expect cloud architect-level expertise from a 4-year engineer. However, they do expect practical experience.

Q471. What Is AWS?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

AWS (Amazon Web Services) is a cloud computing platform providing infrastructure and managed services.

Q472. Why Use Cloud Instead Of Physical Servers?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

  • Elastic Scaling
  • Pay As You Go
  • High Availability
  • Reduced Maintenance
  • Global Reach

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

  • EC2
  • RDS
  • S3
  • ECS
  • EKS
  • Lambda
  • CloudWatch

Q474. What Is EC2?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Elastic Compute Cloud provides virtual machines in AWS.

Q475. What Is RDS?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Managed relational database service.

Examples

  • PostgreSQL
  • MySQL
  • MariaDB
Q476. Why RDS Instead Of Self-Managed Database?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Automated Backups
  • Patching
  • Monitoring
  • High Availability
  • Reduced Operational Work
Q477. What Is S3?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Object storage service.

Use Cases

  • File Uploads
  • Documents
  • Images
  • Backups
Q478. What Is CloudWatch?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

AWS monitoring and observability service.

Tracks

  • Metrics
  • Logs
  • Alarms
  • Dashboards
Q479. What Is AWS Secrets Manager?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Secure storage for application secrets.

Examples

  • Database Passwords
  • API Keys
  • JWT Secrets

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer


GitHub

↓

CI/CD

↓

↓

ECR

↓

ECS/EKS

↓

SECTION 83 - DOCKER

Q481. What Is Docker?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Docker is a containerization platform.

It packages applications with dependencies into containers.

Q482. Why Docker?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Consistency
  • Portability
  • Isolation
  • Easy Deployment
Q483. Virtual Machine vs Docker

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

VM Docker
Full OS Shares Host Kernel
Heavier Lighter
Slower Startup Fast Startup
Q484. What Is A Docker Image?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Immutable blueprint used to create containers.

Q485. What Is A Docker Container?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Running instance of a Docker image.

Q486. Dockerfile Purpose

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Defines instructions for building Docker images.

Q487. Typical Spring Boot Dockerfile

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer


FROM eclipse-temurin:21

COPY app.jar app.jar

ENTRYPOINT ["java","-jar","app.jar"]
Q488. Docker Volumes

ROI: High ★★★★☆

Answer

Persistent storage for containers.

Q489. Why Containers Became Popular For Microservices?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Fast Deployment
  • Consistency
  • Easy Scaling
  • Environment Independence
Q490. Production Scenario: Container Keeps Restarting. What Would You Check?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Strong Answer

  • Container Logs
  • Memory Limits
  • Startup Failures
  • Health Checks
  • Configuration Errors

SECTION 84 - KUBERNETES

Q491. What Is Kubernetes?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Kubernetes is a container orchestration platform.

Responsibilities

  • Deployment
  • Scaling
  • Self Healing
  • Service Discovery
Q492. Why Kubernetes?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

  • Automated Deployment
  • Scaling
  • Resilience
  • Self Healing
Q493. What Is A Pod?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes.

Contains one or more containers.

Q494. What Is Deployment?

ROI: Guaranteed ★★★★★

Answer

Manages pod lifecycle and desired state.

Q495. What Is Service In Kubernetes?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Provides stable network access to pods.

Q496. What Is Horizontal Pod Autoscaler?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Interview Ready Answer

Automatically scales pods based on metrics.

Example


CPU > 80%

↓

More Pods
Q497. What Are Liveness And Readiness Probes?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Liveness Readiness
Should Restart? Can Receive Traffic?
Q498. What Happens If Readiness Probe Fails?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Pod remains running but receives no traffic.

Q499. What Happens If Liveness Probe Fails?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Answer

Pod is restarted.

Q500. Production Scenario: Traffic Doubles Suddenly. How Does Kubernetes Help?

ROI: Maximum ★★★★★

Expected Answer

  • Autoscaling
  • Additional Pods
  • Load Distribution
  • Self Healing
